title: To The Rev. A. A. In The Country From His Friend In London.
content:
(AFTER HEINE.)

    Thou little village curate,
         Come quick, and do not wait;
    We'll sit and talk together,
         So sweetly tete-a-tete.

    Oh do not fear the railway
         Because it seems so big--
    Dost thou not daily trust thee
         Unto thy little gig.

    This house is full of painters,
         And half shut up and black;
    But rooms the very snuggest
         Lie hidden at the back.
                Come! come! come!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Horace Smith, titled "To The Rev. A. A. In The Country From His Friend In London.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
